$comment: D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE (C23 6.7.2.2): an enum with an explicit underlying integer type `enum Byte : unsigned char` sizes at the underlying's width (sizeof 1, align 1 — NOT the default int 4). A RUNTIME wrap-at-256 store+load (B_BASE(200)+59+argc = 260 -> u8 -> 4) is the empirical proof the underlying flows into codegen (enum stored/loaded at u8 width; mir_to_lir routes enum -> underlying via reprKind at every width + conversion site, no per-underlying fork). exit = wrapped(4) + sizeof(enum Byte)(1) + sizeof(struct Pair)(2) + offsetof(Pair,b)(1) + 34 = 42; a default-int enum would give a different exit. argc is a runtime arg so the wrap is not const-folded even under release. Data-model-independent, so the one exit code holds on all four targets incl. the shipped release pipeline. Rides D-PARSE-SPECULATIVE-OPTIONAL (the `enum <tag> :` prefix is shared with the anonymous enum bit-field `enum C : 4;`).
